data.title: Oil Above $90, Pump Above $4 — And 7 Energy Stocks Still Trading At A Wide Discount
data.description:
Something in the market’s math doesn’t hold.

The IEA’s director general Fatih Birol has described the current situation at the Strait of Hormuz as the worst energy crisis in history. Oil – which is trading above $90 a barrel – is still 38% above where it traded the day before the conflict began.

At $4.09 per gallon nationally, and $5.86 in California, the pump is not signaling resolution.

Yet seven major energy producers and refiners — the companies that drill the oil, refine the gasoline, and collect the margin — are trading as if Hormuz is already open, the crisis is resolved, and crude is heading back to $65.

Their forward price-to-earnings multiples sit between 7x and 11x, roughly half the S&P 500’s consensus forward P/E of around 22x.

The disconnect is not subtle. It is structural.

The Energy Stocks That Didn’t Get The Memo

The State Street Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF (NYSE:XLE) is up 27% year-to-date, which sounds impressive until you consider that crude oil is up 38% from pre-war levels.

The sector has underperformed its own commodity — and pulled back 10% from its March peak — even as the underlying supply disruption has not materially improved.

That compression is where the opportunity, and the risk, lives.

The April drawdown is the mechanism that created this entry point.

Every name in the table has sold off between 5% and 14% month-to-date, even as WTI has held around $90 and Brent has pushed toward $95. 

Forward price-to-earnings — next-twelve-months P/E — divides the stock price by the earnings analysts expect the company to produce over the coming year. A lower number means investors are paying less for each dollar of expected profit. At 7x to 11x, these names are priced as though the energy cycle ends soon.

Both names trade below 11x forward earnings despite a supply environment that should structurally support refining margins through the summer driving season.

What’s The Market Actually Pricing In?

Energy stocks have always traded at a discount to the broader market, reflecting the cyclical nature of commodity earnings. But a 50%-plus discount in an environment where oil has been above $80 for more than six weeks — and where there is no immediate resolution to the Strait of Hormuz disruption — is unusual.

The explanation lies in what the market is implicitly pricing. Investors assigning a 7x or 8x multiple to an oil producer are assuming one of two things: either the oil price collapses back toward $60 on a ceasefire, or the earnings cycle peaks before they can compound.

Both are reasonable risks. Neither is certain.

The disconnect is sharpest at APA Corp., which trades at 7.2x forward earnings despite a 49.6% year-to-date return — still the lowest multiple among large-cap U.S. producers.

Devon Energy Corp. shows the most analyst conviction, with a median price target implying 31.6% upside from current levels despite an already strong run.

What This Means For Investors

The valuation gap is a two-sided trade. On one side, if the Hormuz disruption proves durable — no ceasefire, no rerouting solution that meaningfully restores flow — then $90 oil becomes the floor, not the ceiling, and 7x to 11x multiples look like a significant mispricing.

Every dollar of oil above $70 flows almost directly to free cash flow for low-breakeven producers like EOG Resources and Coterra Energy.

On the other side, a rapid ceasefire and Strait reopening could bring oil back toward $65 to $70 within weeks, compressing earnings and making current multiples look less attractive in hindsight.

The market is not wrong to embed that optionality — it is simply the question of which scenario deserves more weight.

Seven of the cheapest stocks in the S&P 500 right now happen to be in the sector with the most direct exposure to the defining macro story of 2026.

Whether that is an opportunity or a trap depends entirely on a waterway 7,000 miles from Wall Street.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning Sprott Physical Silver Stock In The Last 5 Years
data.description:
Sprott Physical Silver (NYSE:PSLV) has outperformed the market over the past 5 years by 11.44% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 22.62%. Currently, Sprott Physical Silver has a market capitalization of $16.35 billion.

Buying $100 In PSLV: If an investor had bought $100 of PSLV stock 5 years ago, it would be worth $276.89 today based on a price of $25.64 for PSLV at the time of writing.

Sprott Physical Silver's Performance Over Last 5 Years

data.title: Netflix Earnings Prediction Market Preview: What Will Leadership Say?
data.description:
Wall Street expects EPS of 78 cents on revenue of $12.17 billion, up 15.5% year-over-year.

The company has topped EPS estimates in recent quarters, which is why Polymarket gives a 95% chance of another beat tonight.

Kalshi has a mention market, where traders are betting real money on which specific words Co-CEOs Ted Sarandos, Greg Peters, and CFO Spence Neumann will say on the 4:45 p.m. ET call.

The WBD Hangover

“Acquisition” at 92% and “Warner Bros.” at 86% are near-locks. Neumann already told analysts in March that Netflix would “move forward” with “$2.8 billion in our pocket that we didn’t have a few weeks ago.”

“Paramount” sits at 53%. A direct mention of the rival now absorbing HBO, CNN, and the Warner studio would signal Sarandos is willing to frame the competitive landscape head-on.

JPMorgan upgraded Netflix to Overweight after the walkaway, and the stock surged roughly 15% after the deal collapsed.

Live Sports Is The New Pillar

“Live Event” tops the board at 94%. Netflix has aggressively expanded into live programming over the past two years, with a 10-year, $5 billion WWE Raw deal, Christmas Day NFL games, the record-breaking Paul-Tyson and Paul-Joshua boxing broadcasts, and exclusive US rights to the 2027 and 2031 FIFA Women’s World Cups.

The new three-year MLB package opened on March 25 with Yankees-Giants at Oracle Park. The Home Run Derby and Field of Dreams Game are still to come this year.

“MLB” at 86% and “WWE” at 64% track the existing rights packages.

The strategic read is in how Sarandos frames it.

Netflix has consistently described its live approach as “the drama of sport” rather than owning full league rights, with a preference for appointment-viewing events that drive ad tier engagement.

The Ad Story Is Mispriced

“Ad-Supported” sits at just 55%, up 12.

Ad revenue doubled to roughly $1.5 billion in 2025 and Co-CEO Greg Peters has guided to $3 billion in 2026, with every major preview naming ads as the single most important growth driver for the year.

The low percentage could reflect mention market mechanics.

Management is all but certain to discuss the ads business, but may use “advertising,” “ads,” or “ad tier” rather than the specific “ad-supported” phrasing.

“Price Change / Price Point” at 84% reflects Netflix’s late-March price hike on the standard and ad tiers.

The Silence Around Subscribers

“Subscriber” is at 41%, down 5.

Netflix stopped routinely disclosing membership numbers in Q1 2025. The Street has accepted that subs are no longer the headline KPI, and traders are now pricing ARM, ad load, and operating margin as the metrics that matter.

“Hollywood” at 34% underlines the frame. For the company that now captures 9% of all US TV time, management is expected to keep talking about members and content, not the industry.

The call is at 4:45 p.m. ET.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: The Trade Desk Stock Rises As Ad-Tech Rebound Builds
data.description:
• Trade Desk stock is surging to new heights today. Why is TTD stock up today?

Trade Desk Rebound Builds As Investors Weigh Audit Fallout

TTD investor are continuing to watch fallout after Publicis advised clients to halt spending on the platform over concerns around fee practices, markups, and transparency, even as CEO Jeff Green publicly pushed back.

The Trade Desk is trying to rebuild confidence after Green said, "TTD has not ‘failed' any audit ever," a message that's helped fuel selective dip-buying rather than a broad, clean risk-on surge.

The latest push higher extends a rebound that started near the stock's 52-week low of $19.74, as software and digital advertising platforms lift together when the Nasdaq-100 is steady.

That same higher-beta rotation has kept the stock sensitive to incremental read-throughs on client retention and spend stability, especially with shares still far below prior highs.

Trade Desk Bounce Improves, But Longer-Term Trend Remains Damaged

Trade Desk is still trying to stabilize near the bottom of its 52-week range after April's swing low, which keeps the longer-term chart in "repair mode." The stock is trading 5.8% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) but 24.7% below its 100-day SMA, which indicates the near-term bounce is improving while the intermediate trend remains pressured.

That contrasts with earlier in the week, when The Trade Desk was still 8.3% below its 20-day SMA and 33.7% below its 100-day SMA, underscoring how sharp the snapback has been off the lows. Traders also flagged an RSI reading of 30.99 as consistent with selling pressure easing, but not yet a clean reversal.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, is above its signal line and the histogram is positive, which leans toward selling pressure easing versus the prior downswing.

  • Key Resistance: $22.50 — a nearby area where rebounds have recently struggled to stick.
  • Key Support: $21.00 — a level buyers may defend to keep the bounce intact.

Analysts Stay Bullish On Trade Desk Despite Recent Target Cuts

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $39.67. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Wells Fargo: Equal-Weight (Lowers Target to $24.00) (April 6)
  • Evercore ISI Group: Outperform (Lowers Target to $32.00) (March 19)
  • Rosenblatt: Downgraded to Neutral (Lowers Target to $25.00) (March 18)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for The Trade Desk,, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

TTD Shares Edge Higher Thursday

TTD Stock Price Activity: Trade Desk shares were up 3.31% at $23.10 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Capstone Shares Rise On Q4 Report, Strong Revenue Outlook
data.description:
Capstone Holding Corp. (NASDAQ:CAPS) shares are trading higher Thursday after the company reported fourth-quarter financial results and issued fiscal-year 2026 revenue guidance above estimates.

  • Capstone shares are powering higher. Why is CAPS stock up today?

Q4 Results Come Up Short

Capstone reported an adjusted loss of $1.00 per share, missing the consensus estimate for loss of 13 cents per share. In addition, the company reported revenue of $12.76 million, missing the consensus estimate of $12.80 million.

Capstone said it closed fiscal-year 2025 with approximately $46.9 million in revenue, with gross margin expanding to 23%. The company attributed its performance to acquisition-driven scale and a higher-margin product mix.

Capstone completed two acquisitions during the year, expanding from one to three operating subsidiaries and from four to nine locations across 38 U.S. states and Canada.

The company said the acquired businesses were integrated immediately upon closing, with early synergies supporting margin expansion and operating efficiency.

Operational Highlights And Outlook

Capstone said its expanded platform includes a broader portfolio of premium brands, including Eldorado Stone, enabling cross-sell opportunities and supporting a higher-margin product mix.

The company also identified cost savings from facility consolidation and noted additional efficiencies from logistics optimization and inventory centralization.

Capstone anticipates fiscal-year 2026 revenue of $72.10 million, versus the consensus estimate of $71.61 million, per Benzinga Pro.

Capstone Shares Power Higher

CAPS Price Action: At the time of writing, Capstone shares are trading 11.88% higher at approximately 66 cents, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock.com
